bull.b: Robust 55% ORR in a heavily pretreated gynecologic cohort provides durable proof-of-concept for the CDH6 ADC program. This clinical signal materially raises the probability of successful downstream trials, partnership interest, and eventual revenue pathways, supporting medium-term pipeline value.

bull.t: Low Leverage And Modest Debt Burden
bull.b: Modest debt levels reduce refinancing and interest-service risk for a cash-burning clinical-stage company. Low leverage preserves financial flexibility to pursue trials or partnerships and mitigates solvency pressure over the next several quarters compared with highly leveraged peers.

A materially narrower operating loss trend signals improving expense control or development efficiency. Sustained reduction in burn rate can extend runway, lower near-term financing needs, and strengthen negotiating positions with partners or investors over the medium term.

bear.t: No Product Revenue; Sustained Cash Burn
bear.b: Absence of product revenue combined with ~-$50M TTM negative operating/free cash flow means the company depends on external financing. Persistent cash burn increases fundraising frequency and dilutive pressure, and creates execution risk if capital markets tighten or trials require more funding.

A runway that extends only into early 2027 creates a near-term funding cliff. Management must secure new capital or partners within months, which can force dilution, delay programs, or constrain trial expansion—each materially affecting long-term development timelines and commercialization prospects.

bear.t: Dilution Risk From Incentive Plan And Eroded Equity
bear.b: Expanding the equity award pool amid a dramatically reduced equity base raises material dilution risk for existing holders. Combined with recurring financings typical for clinical-stage firms, increased share issuance can meaningfully dilute future economics and shareholder claims on eventual revenues.
